Functions for CRC32 and CRC8 calculations on byte streams, with initialization and incremental support.

# Purpose
This C header file provides function declarations for calculating cyclic redundancy checks (CRC) on data. It includes functions for initializing CRC calculations with [`crc_init`](<#crc_init>), computing a 32-bit CRC for a byte stream using [`crc_calculate_bytes`](<#crc_calculate_bytes>), and calculating a 32-bit CRC for data stored in flash memory with [`crc_calculate_flash`](<#crc_calculate_flash>). Additionally, it offers a function [`crc8_calculate_bytes`](<#crc8_calculate_bytes>) to compute an 8-bit CRC for a byte sequence, which uses a non-standard polynomial. The file also declares functions [`crc_calculate_incremental_start`](<#crc_calculate_incremental_start>) and [`crc_calculate_incremental_stop`](<#crc_calculate_incremental_stop>) to manage incremental CRC calculations. The functions are not safe to call from an interrupt service routine (ISR).
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdint.h`
